summary: Keep Windows-only native-engine imports and test helpers out of non-Windows Clippy builds after the first hosted M2 candidate exposed three Linux-only unused-item diagnostics.

decisions.topic: Cross-platform ownership of Windows-only native helpers
decisions.decision: Compile the shim-descriptor filesystem import, Win32 error assertion type, and Win32 project-layout fixture helper only on Windows while leaving the portable path-layout test available on every target.
decisions.rationale: The first hosted Linux Clippy run correctly rejected items used only by Windows implementations and tests. Target-gating those items preserves the Windows security cases without weakening the portable test or suppressing warnings.

testEvidence.criterion: Windows-only native items do not fail non-Windows warning-denied builds
testEvidence.test: Use the hosted Ubuntu Clippy failure as the platform-specific RED, add only target ownership attributes, and keep the local Windows all-target Clippy lane warning-free before requesting one replacement hosted run.

red.command: GitHub Actions CI run 29436756346, Native checks / Rust lint
red.outcome: RED with exit code 101: Ubuntu Clippy under -D warnings reported unused std::fs and crate::EngineError imports plus dead project_with test helper; the other three hosted jobs passed.

green.outcome: GREEN on the pinned Windows Rust 1.96.0 toolchain with exit code 0 after 2.40 seconds. This proves the attributes preserve the Windows all-target lane; the replacement hosted Ubuntu result remains required as the non-Windows acceptance evidence.

questions: The exact replacement candidate still requires one successful hosted Ubuntu Native checks result; this ledger entry does not predeclare that external result.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
